title: RNA agarose gel electrophoresis with RNA sample buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c0f41","text":"This protocol describes how to use formamide-based RNA loading dye for gel electrophoresis in standard agarose-gels in TAE (or sodium borate) buffer to assess their size and quality after in vitro transcriptions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":188,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cqe7e","text":"All quantities and volumes in this protocol are for a 100-mL 1% agarose gel. This concentration works fine for RNA \u003e500 nt, for shorter RNA, go up to 2.5% of agarose. Bands \u003c500 nt will be increasingly diffuse. The TAE buffer can be substituted by a sodium borate buffer (for the gel and the electrophoresis chamber) which will yield sharper bands (see Materials section). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ej9h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsuop","text":"Although, the RNA is somewhat protected from RNases upon adding the loading dye, it is best to still keep it on ice and load it on the gel in a reasonable time (do not wait hours to load). Theoretically, the RNA can be degrated by RNases (in the gel, running buffer, surfaces with contact to the buffer) in the gel so Thermo Scientific recommends working with RNase-free reagents and equipment, but practically, we haven't seen a problem when running the gels like we would do for DNA, just replace the buffer in the chamber with fresh one (not specially treated or anything) before the run.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ahha","text":"Have the RNA sample ready on ice or in a benchtop cooler.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7gpr","text":"SAFETY REMARKS: Formamide (main component of the RNA loading dye) is a toxic chemical, wear gloves and follow safety regulations. Be careful when handling hot liquids and be extra cautious when removing the agarose solution from the microwave, it might have been superheated and boil over upon agitation. Wear heat resistant gloves, eye protection and general PPE. Ethidium bromide is a suspected carcinogen, wear gloves when handling it or the gels, buffers and chambers it has been in contact with, it's best practice to have a dedicated work area for ethidium bromide. Dispose of hazardous chemicals according to your institution's chemical waste guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"86m6l","text":"50× TAE electrophoresis buffer:          for 1 L:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":49}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74hkj","text":"2 M       Tris free base                      242 g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"331ts","text":"50 mM  Disodium EDTA                   18.61 g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9jfj1","text":"1 M        Glacial Acetic Acid              57.1 ml","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s7cj","text":"Dissolve the Tris and EDTA in 700 mL of dH2O under vigorous stirring. Add the acetic acid to the solution and fill the volume to 1 L with water. The pH should be around 8.2-8.5 and doesn't need adjustment. Sterilize by autoclaving or filtration (0.2 µm pore size) and store at room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":42,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4c0bh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6hk06","text":"1× TAE electrophoresis buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4b00c","text":"40mM  Tris","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ctfq","text":"20mM  Acetate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2blro","text":"1mM     EDTA","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6km6b","text":"For 1 L, mix 20 mL 50× buffer stock with 980 mL dH2O. The pH will slightly increase and should be around 8.6 (again, no adjustment needed). Store at room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":50,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aps2k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fql58","text":"Agarose, electrophoresis-grade ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9p9rj","text":"Invitrogen™ UltraPure™ Agarose - 16500500","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87kal","text":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/16500500","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":59}],"data":{}},{"key":"rpl5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2i1m","text":"2× RNA gel loading dye:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4g6b","text":"Thermo Scientific™ 2X RNA Loading Dye - R0641 (plenty supplied with ladders)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajtvm","text":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/R0641?SID=srch-srp-R0641","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":75}],"data":{}},{"key":"12fim","text":"This dye contains 95% formamide, 0.025% SDS, 0.5 mmol/L EDTA, 0.025% bromophenol blue, 0.025% xylene cyanol FF and 0.025 % ethidium bromide. The pH will increase and should be around 8.5 (no adjustment needed). Store at room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":50,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"91g6s","text":"2.5% agarose gels can be run at 10 V/cm or even higher and yield great separation and clear bands especially below 500 nt or bp. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b039h","text":"The only caveat is that the running behavior is sensitive to high salt or glycerol concentrations in the loaded sample, but for kit-purified RNA and the formamide-based RNA loading dye, that is no problem.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":205}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"3F2248884C9011F094360A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/16500500"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"44FE20494B3D11F0936B0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/R0641?SID=srch-srp-R0641"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5599C4EA4B3D11F0BED10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/SM1821"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6FE85D1C4B3D11F0BED10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/SM1831"}},"4":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"958722284C9411F094360A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.idtdna.com/pages/products/reagents-and-kits/buffers-and-solutions"}},"5":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"518A52064C9011F0ACA50A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/US/en/product/sigma/e1510"}}}}
steps: For 100 mL of a 1% agarose gel, add 1 g agarose to a glass flask with 300-500 mL capacity.
Use gel immediately or store moisture-sealed at 4°C for weeks.
Pour the gel into an appropriate gel tray with a fitting comb and let it solidify for 30 min. This can be accelerated if the gel tray is placed at 4°C.
OPTIONAL: Add dye for RNA detection (e.g., ethidium bromide) to the desired concentration (50-100 µL from a 10 mg/mL ethidium bromid solution for 100 mL gel).
Place on a stirring plate to mix and let cool down to ~60°C. The stirring speed should be high enough to fully mix the solution but not high enough to introduce air bubbles.
Carefully take out the hot flask and check that the agarose has dissolved completely. For higher agarose percentages, additional 1 or 2 min might be required to fully dissolve the agarose.
Microwave at a medium setting for 5 min. This helps prevent the liquid from boiling over. Higher power might dissolve the agarose quicker but requires constant attention and intervention.
Take a wettened, crumbled paper towel and use it to loosely plug the flask. This just helps with overboiling and evaporation in the next step.
Add 100 mL of 1× TAE buffer to the flask and add a stirring bar.

For a 1-µL RNA sample in a PCR tube, add 2 µL nuclease-free water and 3 µL 2× RNA loading dye. 1 µL of RNA is plenty for highly concentrated in vitro transcribed RNA, for lower concentration, increase the volume and decrease the water volume.
Transfer 6 µL of the appropriate RNA ladder (same volume and RNA loading dye as the sample) into another PCR tube.

Denature sample and ladder at 70°C for 10 min in a PCR cycler. This denaturing step helps with resolving at least some secondary structures, so in the best case, the RNA runs in a single band, but multiple bands are still a possibility.
Immediately cool down to 4°C in the cycler or on ice for 5 min and keep at that temperature until loading the gel.
Thaw 2× RNA loading dye and an appropriate RNA ladder (in the same dye) on ice. Once thawed, vortex both and quickly spin down before placing them on ice again.
Fill the electrophoresis tank with fresh 1×TAE. If using ethidium bromide for RNA detection without post-run staining, add 50 µL per liter buffer at the anode of the tank.
Load all of the denatured ladder and sample into the wells.
Run the gel at a constant 3-5 V/cm for 20-30 min. Higher percentage gels need longer for a good separation.

OPTIONAL: For highest quality images or if no ethidium bromide (or alternative dyes) were added to the gel and buffer, do your routine gel staining/destaining procedure (depending on the dye used, same as for DNA gels).

Image gel and check for signs of RNA degradation (marked smears below the bands or blobs on the bottom of the gel, see example below). Example of two (partially) degraded RNA samples on a 1% agarose, TAE+bleach gel and DNA reagents . The RNA ladder should give a good estimate of the sizes and with the denaturing RNA loading dye, each RNA should only give the expected band. If signs of degradation are visible, think about re-running another sample on a TAE+bleach gel (with DNA ladder and loading dyes; unfortunately, the formamide-based RNA loading dye is not compatible with bleach gels): https://www.protocols.io/private/2D3DA5744C9611F094360A58A9FEAC02
creator: Christian CR Renicke
